title: Add Users REST API Endpoint
description:
## Context

A REST endpoint is needed to programmatically add additional users to a tenant after a Stripe purchase. This endpoint is called by Zapier after a successful Stripe payment and will also be integrated into Salesforce for manual admin provisioning.

## Behavior

Create a REST endpoint at `/api/v1_0/addusers` that accepts a team ID (public-facing unique ID) and a number of users to add. The endpoint validates the request using a bearer token and increases the team's user quota.

## Security Model

The API is restricted to internal system use only — it is not exposed to end users. Access is controlled via admin-level bearer tokens, and the endpoint is locked down using AWS-level restrictions so that only DataTools Pro systems can call it. Public-facing unique IDs are used instead of internal integer IDs to prevent enumeration attacks.

## Request Format

```
POST /api/v1_0/addusers
Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>
{
  "team_id": "590E29C08085",
  "user_number": 4
}
```
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1_0/addusers endpoint accepts team_id and user_number in request body
Endpoint uses public-facing unique team ID, not internal integer ID
Only admin-level tokens are accepted — regular user tokens are rejected
Endpoint increases the team's user quota by the specified number
Endpoint is restricted to internal system access via AWS-level controls
Integration with Salesforce for manual admin provisioning is supported
middleware_changes: New controller logic to validate the bearer token is admin-level, look up the team by public-facing unique ID, and increment the user quota. Initially used a specific user's token for validation, with plans for role-based access control.
api_changes: POST /api/v1_0/addusers — accepts {team_id: string, user_number: number}. Requires admin bearer token. Returns updated user count.
implementation_notes: Initially implemented with internal integer team_id, later refactored to use the public-facing unique ID. The endpoint was first tested via Postman. Security was a key concern: the team discussed two approaches (validate token ownership vs. admin-only access) and chose admin-only with AWS-level lockdown. The endpoint was eventually updated to accept the public-facing unique ID format used in the Stripe client_reference_id (e.g., '590E29C08085').
comment_summary: Extensive security discussion: developer raised concern about team_id enumeration if the endpoint was user-facing. Decision was made to restrict the endpoint to admin-only access with AWS-level lockdown. Later revisited to switch from internal integer IDs to public-facing unique IDs. Final implementation confirmed working with the Stripe payment link's client_reference_id format.
